title: A thin functional watch with smart features
text:
I bought this because it's relatively smaller/thinner plus it offers heart rate and blood pressure tracking (BP comes later). I also like gadgets. I play with the watch a lot so I'm only getting 36 hours on the battery (plan on charging daily). It's thin and fits under my dress shirt cuff.

I've worn this for a few days 24/7 (including, to bed, as well as the shower & gym). It's very comfortable (no itching or rubbing) and performs well. The heart rate tracker is pretty accurate (give it time to adapt to changes in activity) and compares to the heart rate readings I get from the machines in the gym. I was doing my income tax on Sunday and the watch detected and notified me that I was stressed - and offered to walk me through deep breathing exercises (clever).

I like that I can set the notification feature to only allow texts to show on the watch. If it's important I'll respond on my phone (not the watch). And I like that the notification stops when it detects

title: Warranty does NOT cover water damage
text:
The FIRST time I tried to go swimming with this watch it broke. I explained what happened to Samsung support, they said that the repair would be covered under warranty, then once they received the watch tried to charge me $140 to fix a $200 watch! They said this is because the damage was caused by water intrusion, even though there was only water intrusion in this case was due to a manufacturing defect!
So be warned, the watch is "waterproof" but if the one you get isn't it's not Samsung's fault and they wont pay for it!

title: Love love love!!!
text: I've ran through "smart" watches. I had a fossil smart watch and many other health smart watches. This is theeeee best!! I want to buy another just so I always have one if something happened to this one omg. I think the battery life is great. I have a dock so I put it on there at night and it always at 50% when I do that. I keep the face always on. So customizable. You can make this exactly how you want it. The most accurate monthly tracker for women I've ever had. They are usually way off but this has been exact. Spot on. Love this one it's perfect.

title: Smart Watch for Seniors
text: Product does what it was designed, for the price. Perfect starter watch for seniors (HR) or smart watch beginners.

title: Gorgeous watch
text:
It's a gorgeous watch! Just what I wanted ❤️ not so big, discrete and does what I need for an amazing price. A little scared the screen will get cracked or scratched, will need to buy a protector.

Edit:I didn't know it doesn't have a speaker, not so bothered by it but I didn't know.

*There's a lack of apps for tizen... Not so happy about it.

title: Great Watch
text:
Bought this for my daughter for her birthday. She has been wanting a watch to track her steps and have a watch. We are a Samsung family and she deserved a great gift. Having a Samsung S2 watch, I knew of several ways to save battery life and to set up the watch.
Battery Life: So far she is hitting about 60% by the end of the day as she uses. I have the screen brightness set to 5 or 6. I also taught her how to turn on and off the "Wake up gesture" which can activate while walking, running, or moving around, which can drain the battery life.
Set Up: Unfortunately, that took a little bit longer than I had planned. However, that was my fault, not the watches. I simply pulled it out, turned it on, and then worked on connecting it to my daughter's Samsung Note 8. I did not pull out the directions or even look at them. Even still, I knew the basis of what needed to be done. I muddled through it and had it connected in about 10 minutes. (Probably could have cut that in half if I had

title: LOVE it!
text: I got this today and really love how it looks and feels so far.  It works great too, it has the same functionality as the Gear Sport as far as I can tell and is .1 smaller on the screen size with no rotating bezel.  It will be more comfortable to sleep in than the Sport I had before.  You can't take calls on it but can talk into text just like the Gear Sport and it seems pretty accurate and keeps the text history.  There's nothing about it I don't like so far but I'll come back and update if I discover something.  The charger is a USB so I can use my phone charger, computer or car and the part that attaches to the watch is as small as the watch face and is a magnet to click on the back.  For the price this is a fantastic smart watch but as the Gear Sport comes down in price you can choose between the 2 based on if you just want a rotating bezel or not... I don't miss it I didn't really use it that much anyway.

title: Atrocious battery life, mediocre performance
text:
You would be lucky to get an hour out if this. I bought it for running (it has Spotify offline so I don't need to bring my phone): With MapmyRun, Spotify, and my Bluetooth headphones in, I can get two 30 min runs out of it. Do NOT buy if you're planning to do any excercise for more than an hour.

The device itself is also pretty sluggish. Not terrible given its mostly single purpose for me, but the experience wasn't great as a watch in general. But mostly I am just confused as to why Samsung released a product aimed at activity and included such a weak battery.

title: Love it, wish it had longer battery life
text: Overall I love this watch and I would purchase it again. My only complaint is the battery life. It claims to last 3 days but that must be if you disable all the features. Mine lasts 24 hours and I charge it every night when I get home from work so that I can wear it overnight for the sleep tracker. I have always on disabled and sense workout disabled and wifi disabled, and it gave me an extra hour or two of battery life. It's great in every other way and I love that you can control Spotify on it and that it connects to Samsung health on my phone.

title: Low Battery Life, Difficult to Handle Apps
text: I had to charge this watch every single day since I have received it. I am simply appalled by the short battery life. I also can't set its apps properly; for example, it was impossible to add a desired location for the world watch. Very expensive for the little it can do

title: Good looking watch but the 40mm might seem Abit small for some
text:
Update: 8/21/2020- so the battery has been draining VERY quickly the last few weeks. It charges to 100% but then by 4 hours it's at a 43% charges with it just being on my wrist with nothing really running. I've reset the watch, made sure it has the latest updates and turned off a lot of the necessary bloat but no luck. Not sure what to do with it at this time.

6/21/2020: Arrived without a scratch on the watch or band. Charger and extra watch band was also included. Definitely like new. I was a little worried as the packaging was a little grimy. I do have a skinny wrist and I've had a 42mm smartwatch prior (which to me was the perfect size) so putting on a 40mm made it seem "small" even on my skinny wrist. But I found that adding a watch cover "bulked it" up just enough for me so I'm happy!
I cannot stand any of the Samsung related health apps. Honestly I'm only using this watch for running so Im only using the UA app and cell phone-less usage of music. A note b

title: Condition was better than expected!
text:
[[VIDEOID:7aef337a0d7f5f479b2c59067f5a41a]] I was a little concerned when purchasing this only because of the disclaimer of the condition but was honestly thrilled when I pulled it out the box. I haven't had it long enough to comment on the functionality but it definitely gets a thumbs up from me based on looks alone.

Update: After having the watch for some time, I am still very happy. Works fine. Battery lasts. Very happy with my purchase.

title: The good, the bad, and the obviously wrong
text:
I bought the Galaxy Active for two reasons: my Withings HR Steel was undercounting my calories burned, and I wanted to cycle and run without my phone. I really dislike square watches (Apple Watch and most fitness trackers), and I hate bulky watches (all Android watches and the rest of the Samsung line up).

The Active looks great, especially when you replace the band for something nicer, and it is feature packed. Not as many apps as the Apple Watch or even Wear OS, but certainly has a functional app store that covers most of the bases.

Unfortunately this watch has glaring flaws. While my HR Steel was under counting my calories, the Active is all over the place. Sometimes it will track 600 calories for a brisk 4 mile walk, other times the exact same route will track 350 (more in line with reality but still off), however it seems to be pretty accurate with cycling calories (6 mile cycle at about 300 calories).

The worst part about the watch is Spotify. It

title: Terrible battery life
text: Battery life is really bad. I read that but I thought I would try it. I have returned the watch. The battery lasted one and a half days. I would suggest “DONT BUY!

title: Samsung Galaxy sport
text: Shipped super fast. I was surprised that I still use this after many months every day. I know I haven't even tapped into its full potential, but for my need, i.e steps, time, heart rate, emails, texts, social media, calculator, weather, calendar - it's perfect. Everything is right on my wrist. The water proof is a nice feature for being caught in the rain. The band is easily cleaned as it is a soft rubber material. I like the customization of it, too. Well worth the money even for the few apps that I personally use. There are so many more available for other users.

title: Easy to Use!
text: This is my 3rd smart watch. I have had a Kate Spade and Michael Kors one. Those both used Wear OS. In comparison to those watches, I like this one better. It did require me to download more apps on my phone such as Galaxy Wearable. This watch is easy to use. There are 2 controls. The Michael Kors one had 2 controls, too, but their location was always accidently getting bumped which sometimes caused it to restart when I didn't want it to. I have never accidently bumped these buttons. The strap that comes with it barely fits my wrist, but there's an extra longer one that comes with it. It comes with a charger. The watch claims to stay charged for days. Well, technically that's true. I find that it lasts for 2 days so every other day I need to recharge it. My former watches both had to be charged every night, so I am pleased that this lasts a little longer. As far as the fitness aspect, this is better than my other ones. The step tracking seems to be more parallel to what I used to get fr

title: Can find it cheaper elsewhere.
text: I have a Gear S2 that only lasts half a day. I bought this because it claimed top last 2 days. So far,  the phone lasts for a bit longer than a day,  it seems. However,  2 days later,  I saw the same watch at Samsung for $30 less. Wish I hadn't put it into service-I would have sent it back

title: Simple and comfortable!
text:
I wanted something small enough to resemble a regular watch and simple enough to get a quick notification of my messages.

Wish it had a speaker but that is the only thing from the gear s2 I liked.

title: It's a great device, but software is very buggy
text:
Basic time, weather and notifications - that's all that is working reliably. It's like 10% of what is really advertised.

Half of the times the watch itself cannot remember WiFi Connection, doesn't connect to your WiFi by default, you have to always click Connect to point manually. That leads to a lot of problems with apps like Strava and Spotify that need connection. They just don't sync! Applications won't install. There's always trouble when you switch phones.

You manage your watch with Samsung Galaxy App on iOS. It often doesn't work as a connection provider either. The app says the Watch is connected, the watch says it's connected but there's no Internet on the Watch. So more problems with Strava, Spotify and all apps that need Internet connection.

Samsung Health syncs 50% of the time. It says "No wearables detected". The only thing that Samsung Support recommended is restarting Watch, Bluetooth and iPhone. Really? It never helped.

Bixby

title: False Advertising - Don't Use it in Water, at all
text:
Even though it states that it is water-resistant up to 50 meters, they do not actually warranty it. So if you get water damage (from regular use in a shower or regular swimming, I'm not talking about diving with it), they won't cover it. You'll have to pay for the repair (which is more than the price of the watch at this point).

Also, battery is so so, I expected a bit better honestly as far as battery life went.

title: Wow
text: Didn’t expect it to be better than the fitbit. It’s really much much better than a fitbit.

title: Disappointing
text: Marginally acceptable functionality and a lot of frustration. This is my 1st product of its type, so i cannot compare to anything. Very clumsy and frustrating to operate. Randomly detects and tracks types of exercise. No more than ballpark accuracy detecting & tracking heart rate. Needs to be charged at least once every day. Face is too small for a touch screen . Three star rating is very generous.

title: Battery life too short
text: Battery life is awful. I did not explore but I wanted to remove the Samsung pay app. I had a Fitbit charge 3, lasts almost a week on a single charge, but Galaxy active disappointed me. I returned, but will buy next version if battery life improved and case thickness reduced. Belt could be better designed.

title: Great product
text: Love this smartwatch, very useful when it comes to Spotify. I love that I can use it without having to take my phone out. I also love the voice recognition for texting.

title: Great Watch
text: The watch has been as good as advertised. It was used but looks pretty good. Sellers have been great checking on product as well. 10 🌟

title: Love this watch!
text: Comfortable to wear, great technology, well thought out. Love that it automatically recognizes when I am going for a walk. I set my notifications so I never miss a text since I'm a careerwoman with a family. Its easy to stay in touch and be reminded of important events with this watch.

title: Excellent new version.
text: Have had Gear S2 for 3 years but battery draining quickly. The new version is really great. Even easier to replace strap. Comfortable and excellent low profile watch. Elegant looking.  n

title: Works as Advertised.
text: This is the best digital watch I have ever owned. I have a penchant for watches and I love this Samsung watch. It's never intrusive but gives so much data when you want it.  The manufacter knew what they were doing in producing this WATCH!

title: Old obsolete
text: This is the active. Buy the active 2

title: I like it
text:
It dies what I need, when doing my exercise. Obviously not quite as good as the apple, but if ya don't have an apple phone, no need to have an apple watch.!
The problem I have is heart rate not accurate. And it lises connectivity with phone.
